page: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnv6 server family. It is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server configuration does not include local storage, requiring the use of external Premium Storage, which the instance is capable of supporting. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2ns_v6 provides a balanced entry-level hardware profile for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources without the overhead of local storage. It is suitable for general-purpose workloads, network-optimized tasks, light web serving, and small-scale database applications that rely on remote premium storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine from the Dnv6 family, utilizing an Intel x86_64 processor and the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It features no local storage, relying instead on its Premium Storage capability for external disk volumes. With zero integrated GPUs and no complimentary public IPv4 addresses, the D2ns_v6 is designed as a streamlined, cost-efficient option for entry-level workloads. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, network-optimized tasks, and small-scale deployments that utilize remote storage.
